overview: The Village of Weston Plan Commission approved a site plan for the new Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive, and established a salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection areas. Both items passed unanimously.

agenda.item: Call to order and roll call
Site plan review for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive
Salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection district
April 2026 staff approved certified survey maps and site plans
Planning and development department monthly project update report

discussions.item: Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court Facility Site Plan
discussions.body: Staff presented the site plan for the new police department and municipal court at 3600 Community Center Drive. The building was rotated to face east due to a gas pipeline constraint, which allowed it to be placed on higher ground and reduced fill costs. Chief Jeremy explained the building will accommodate 50-60 officers for future growth, up from current staffing of 40 officers. Discussion included the alternate storage shed placement and potential for future building expansion to the north. Motion to recommend approval to the board of trustees with staff conditions 2-5 passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Salt Storage Shed Inspection Program for Wellhead Protection District
discussions.body: Staff proposed a program with two annual inspections per facility - a warm weather inspection focusing on structural integrity and drainage, and a winter inspection focusing on material handling and containment. Jennifer noted they identified one existing facility near Sternberg that never went through the wellhead protection conditional use process and is closest to the village well. Commissioners agreed to reach out to that facility about compliance. Motion to approve the program with reporting back to both Plan Commission and Public Works passed unanimously.

discussions.item: April 2026 Staff Approved Items and Building Permits
discussions.body: Both the April 2026 staff approved certified survey maps/site plans and the April 2026 building permits were approved unanimously without discussion.

Staff announced the first joint Plan Commission/Village Board steering committee meeting for the comprehensive plan is scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 5:00 p.m. Discussion followed about potential residential TIF districts for workforce housing, with staff noting Ehlers has prepared a presentation on qualifications that could be shared with the commission or incorporated into comprehensive plan discussions.

publicComment: Jim Pensel of 5002 Arrow Street, Weston, spoke via online connection. He urged the commission to exercise common sense on landscaping requirements for the police department project, referencing relaxed standards applied to the Greenheck Turner building. He also requested that the salt storage shed inspection program include annual reporting back to the commission on site visits and findings.
actionItems: Site plan for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department approved with conditions 2-5, forwarded to Board of Trustees
Salt storage shed inspection program approved for implementation in wellhead protection district
Staff to report inspection findings to both Plan Commission and Public Works committee next year
Staff to reach out to existing salt storage facility near Sternberg about wellhead protection compliance
Joint Plan Commission/Village Board steering committee meeting for comprehensive plan scheduled for June 1st at 5:00 p.m.
Staff to send email and calendar invite for June 1st steering committee meeting
Electric vehicle charging station item added to future agenda for fall
Next regular meeting scheduled for Monday, June 8, 2026 at 6:00 p.m.
title: Plan Commission
